text: Fence experiments and side projects off from the main onboarding path
description: Make sure `subdev/` and other non-core experiments do not confuse first-time users. This can mean docs clarifications, repo structure notes, or other lightweight measures that keep the release story centered on the main product.

reflections.context: Reduce first-time-user confusion by fencing experiments and side projects away from the main Aimparency onboarding path.
reflections.outcome: Added subdev/README.md and updated the root README so subdev is explicitly framed as exploratory material rather than part of the main product surface.
reflections.effectiveness: A lightweight docs boundary was enough for this pass because the main issue was discoverability and framing, not code behavior.
reflections.lesson: For open source release prep, boundary-setting docs often solve confusion faster than restructuring directories.
reflections.pattern: Similar to package README cleanup, small documentation changes can materially improve repo legibility without risky code changes.

status.comment: Added subdev/README.md and tightened root docs so experiments and side projects are explicitly outside the main Aimparency onboarding path.
